answer.recommended_action: Physically inspect and clean the transmit/receive optical window. ev_spatial_intensity_001 shows a localized, non-uniform intensity shortfall (spread=0.484, localized=true) consistent with a discrete surface contaminant — smear, film, or debris — causing partial aperture obscuration. All uniform-effect mechanisms are eliminated: thermal regulation is healthy with headroom (ev_tec_load_check_001), no config/firmware parameters changed (ev_config_diff_001), and H2/H3 predict spatially uniform attenuation which is contradicted by the high inter-region spread. The recent reboot is a non-causal coincident event (ev.trigger_demoted) since degradation onset predates it.

hypotheses.label: Window contamination reducing transmitted/received optical power (~8% SNR loss consistent with partial obscuration or film buildup)
Laser output power aging/degradation reducing peak emitted intensity over multi-week timescale
Detector responsivity drift (dark-current rise or gain shift) elevating noise floor and shrinking effective range
TEC/thermal regulation failure causing elevated or unstable operating temperature that degrades both laser and detector performance
Calibration or firmware parameter drift (range-gate offset, threshold coefficient, or intensity scaling) producing apparent range shortfall without hardware fault

evidence.summary: TEC current is 1.19 A, only 48% of the 2.5 A limit; diode temperature is 25.15 °C, within 0.85 °C of the 26 °C setpoint ceiling and not losing setpoint. Thermal regulation is healthy with ample headroom — no thermal runaway, no saturation, no temperature instability.

config_diff returned no changed parameters and any_change=false. No firmware update, range-gate offset change, threshold coefficient edit, or intensity-scaling modification is on record. The apparent 8 % range shortfall therefore cannot be attributed to a deliberate or accidental parameter write; however, internal runtime state drift not persisted to config files remains a residual (lower-probability) possibility.

Spatial intensity check across 4 aperture regions yields spread=0.484 and localized=true. The intensity shortfall is non-uniform across the aperture, which is the signature of a discrete optical surface contaminant or partial obscuration (window film, smear, debris) rather than any system-wide degradation mechanism. Laser output aging (H2) and detector responsivity drift (H3) would each produce spatially uniform attenuation with near-zero inter-region spread; the high spread and localized flag argue against both. TEC/thermal failure (H4) and calibration drift (H5) are similarly uniform-effect mechanisms and are further disfavoured.
log.reboot: degradation onset predates it → coincident, not causal

trace.rationale: seed the differential from the symptom

All five hypotheses are at equal prior (log_odds 0.00) and no evidence has been collected yet. The TEC/thermal check is the highest-leverage first step because H4 (thermal regulation failure) is a root-cause hypothesis that, if confirmed, would simultaneously explain H2 (laser power degradation) and H3 (detector noise rise) as thermally-driven secondary effects — collapsing three hypotheses into one. Conversely, a nominal TEC result (stable setpoint, low duty-cycle, no oscillation) provides strong evidence AGAINST H4 and decouples H2/H3 from a thermal driver, forcing independent hardware or software explanations. H1 (window contamination) and H5 (calibration drift) are both thermally-independent, so TEC behaviour does not confound them. This single check therefore produces the greatest log-odds separation across the leading set: an abnormal result raises H4 sharply and pulls H2/H3 upward as co-effects, while a clean result drops H4 and redirects attention to H1, H2 (intrinsic aging), H3 (intrinsic drift), and H5. No evidence has been cited yet; this action is proposed to generate the first discriminating datum.

H1 (window contamination) and H5 (calibration/firmware drift) are co-leading at log_odds=0.00. H4 is already ruled out by ev_tec_load_check_001. config_diff is the sharpest single discriminator between the two leaders: if range-gate offsets, threshold coefficients, or intensity-scaling parameters show a change whose timestamp aligns with the ~1-week onset, H5 rises substantially and hardware hypotheses H1/H2/H3 become secondary; if the diff is clean (no parameter mutations), H5 is effectively eliminated and all residual probability shifts to optical/hardware causes. This binary software-vs-hardware fork is cheaper and more unambiguous than any hardware measurement taken while H5 remains unrefuted — acting on H1 or H2/H3 before ruling out a miscalibration would risk unnecessary physical intervention. H2 and H3 (both at log_odds=-1.00) are not advanced here because a clean diff will surface them naturally as the next tier.

H4 is ruled out by ev_tec_load_check_001 and H5 by ev_config_diff_001. The remaining contest is H1 (window contamination) vs H2 (laser aging) vs H3 (detector drift). A spatial_intensity_check is the single most discriminating step: (a) spatially non-uniform or azimuthally patterned intensity reduction → strongly raises H1 and lowers H2/H3, because optical hardware degradation and detector drift are isotropic whereas contamination is not; (b) spatially uniform ~8% intensity drop across all channels → lowers H1, raises H2 (laser power decline) and leaves H3 viable; (c) intensity nominally flat but with elevated per-channel noise variance → favours H3 (dark-current / gain shift) over H1 and H2. No prior evidence has addressed the spatial distribution of the optical loss, so this check produces genuinely new discriminating information rather than re-confirming already-supported hypotheses.
order onset vs the salient recent event; sanity-check channels
conclude or abstain from the differential
